The word PAMPA, of Araucanian origin, signifies _grass plain_, and justly applies to the whole region. The mimosas growing on the western part, and the substantial herbage on the eastern, give those plains a peculiar appearance. The soil is composed of sand and red or yellow clay, and this is covered by a layer of earth, in which the vegetation takes root. The geologist would find rich treasures in the tertiary strata here, for it is full of antediluvian remains--enormous bones, which the Indians attribute to some gigantic race that lived in a past age.

Captain Hatteras is the monomaniacal explorer of *[[the-adventures-of-captain-hatteras|The Adventures of Captain Hatteras]]*, a man consumed by a single ambition. He conceals his identity until, aboard the mutinous *[[the-forward|Forward]]*, he reveals himself with the declaration, "I am Captain Hatteras!" (ACH-012-041). From that moment the expedition belongs wholly to him and to the goal that governs his life: to attain the Pole was the aim of his life (ACH-012-056).
